prefers-reduced-motion guard — hides steam and freezes liquid at scaleY(0.6)
transform-origin: 50px 88px on .c13-liquid (fills from the bottom up)
knownLimitations: Machine path is hardcoded SVG geometry — resizing the illustration requires adjusting the viewBox and all path coordinates
Fill animation uses scaleY which clips to a rectangle — not a true liquid-level curve

items.title: Fruit Orange
items.description: SVG orange cross-section: 8 wedge segments pulse amber-to-orange in a sequential wave while the whole slice rotates slowly on an 8s spin.
items.useWhen: Playful loading indicators for food, health, or consumer apps. Anywhere a fruit motif or warm radial spinner fits the brand.
items.avoidWhen: Corporate or enterprise contexts. Background hardcoded dark — needs CSS override for light themes.
items.agentPrompt: Change to a lemon by updating .c14-peel stroke to #facc15 and both fill values in c14-pulse keyframe from #f59e0b/#ff781f to #fef08a/#facc15. Stop rotation by setting animation: none on .c14-orange. Reduce to 6 segments by deleting the last two nth-child rules and two path elements.
editableAreas: segment pulse colors (#f59e0b and #ff781f inside c14-pulse keyframe)
pulse wave delay increment (0.2s per nth-child)
segment count (nth-child rules + matching path elements in SVG)
preserveElements: transform-origin: 60px 60px on .c14-segment — rotation pivot at SVG center; wrong value breaks the wave
visibilitychange pause handler for both .c14-orange and all .c14-segment nodes
knownLimitations: All 8 segment path elements share the identical d attribute — position comes from nth-child rotate transforms only; adding a 9th segment requires both a new path element and a new nth-child rule
Background hardcoded in body CSS

items.title: Newton's Cradle
items.description: Five pendulum wires hang from a purple bar; the leftmost (cyan) and rightmost (pink) balls swing alternately with a 0.6s phase offset, simulating momentum transfer.
items.useWhen: Loading states that benefit from a physics or computation-in-progress metaphor. Developer tools, data-processing or simulation apps.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts narrower than 180px width. Confirm the static middle balls read correctly as intentional — they can look broken if the context is unfamiliar.
items.agentPrompt: Change ball colors by editing .c15-wire::after background and box-shadow rgba for cyan balls, and .c15-wire:nth-child(5)::after for the pink ball. Increase swing angle by changing 35deg in both c15-swing-l and c15-swing-r keyframes. Speed up by reducing 1.2s animation duration.
editableAreas: cyan ball color (::after background + box-shadow rgba on .c15-wire)
swing angle (35deg in c15-swing-l and c15-swing-r keyframes)
preserveElements: animation-delay: 0.6s on .c15-wire:nth-child(5) — exactly half the cycle; changing this breaks the cradle illusion
transform-origin: top center on .c15-wire — pivot at the bar; changing breaks the swing
knownLimitations: Middle 3 balls are static (CSS-only, no true physics engine). The alternating swing is a visual illusion.
Swing angle above ~50deg breaks the illusion — the balls appear to pass through each other

items.title: Infinity Loop
items.description: Two glowing traces (pink and cyan) chase each other around a lemniscate path using stroke-dashoffset animation; a near-invisible track marks the full path beneath.
items.useWhen: Elegant loading indicators for dark-theme apps. AI/processing metaphors, infinite-process indicators, anywhere the infinity symbol resonates with the product.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (hardcoded dark fill). Very small sizes — trace detail degrades below ~80px width.
items.agentPrompt: Make single-color by removing the .c17-trace--cyan path element and its CSS rule. Adjust trace length with the first dasharray value (22 = short, 50 = half-path). Speed up by reducing 2s on c17-trace-loop. Thicken by increasing stroke-width from 5 to 7.
editableAreas: trace length (22 — first value in stroke-dasharray; max 100 due to pathLength)
track visibility (0.05 rgba — increase to show the full path)
preserveElements: pathLength='100' on all path elements — makes dasharray values path-percentage not pixels; removing it breaks all animation math
animation-delay: -1s on .c17-trace--cyan — creates the chase offset at exactly half-cycle
degenerate close segment 'C 35 20, 35 40, 35 40' at path end — needed for smooth loop with no jump
knownLimitations: pathLength='100' is intentional and must not be changed — see preserveElements
The lemniscate path closes with a degenerate cubic segment; editing the path requires re-testing loop continuity

items.title: Fruit Watermelon

items.title: Magnify Dock

items.title: Hamburger Morph

items.title: Gooey Tab Bar

items.title: Radial Fan Menu
items.description: A core button rotates to an X and fans five glowing cyan icon petals outward in a staggered spring arc; auto-cycles open/close as a demo.
items.useWhen: Floating action buttons, mobile-style radial nav, speed-dial UIs where compact access to 4-6 actions is needed.
items.avoidWhen: More than 6 actions (petals overlap), or contexts requiring always-visible navigation — petals are hidden by default.
items.agentPrompt: Change the fan arc from -150 to -30 degrees to -200 to -90 so petals open downward-left, and set R=80 to spread them further apart.
editableAreas: stagger delay (i*45 ms open, (n-i)*30 ms close)
knownLimitations: Auto-cycles every 2.2s by default — remove the loop() call before shipping in production
Arc is hardcoded upper-left quadrant; repositioning requires changing start/end angles

items.title: Slide-in Drawer
items.description: Hamburger icon morphs to an X while a side panel slides in over a dark scrim, revealing four nav items with staggered fade-in animations.
items.useWhen: Mobile-style nav demos, workspace sidebars, or any UI needing a collapsible navigation drawer pattern.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts where persistent navigation is required — the auto-cycle loop must be removed and the drawer wired to real routing before shipping.
items.agentPrompt: Change the nav items to ['Home', 'Analytics', 'Team', 'Billing'], set the panel accent border to rgba(0,234,255,0.45), and increase animation delay step to 100ms.
knownLimitations: Auto-cycles every 2.4s by default — remove loop() call before production use
Drawer width hardcoded at 64% — may clip on very narrow viewports
Nav items use div not anchor tags — add href and keyboard focus styles for real nav

items.title: Underline Follow
items.description: A glowing iris-to-cyan gradient underline slides and resizes to follow hovered nav links; wanders idly across links when unhovered.
items.useWhen: Portfolio or app nav bars needing a polished active-state indicator with ambient motion between interactions.
items.avoidWhen: Nav bars with more than 6 items where the idle wander may feel restless; or any context requiring a truly static nav indicator.

knownLimitations: Idle wander uses offsetLeft/offsetWidth — breaks if nav is not laid out when loop starts
No touch equivalent for hover; on mobile the underline stays on last-active item only
Autonomous idle cycle must be removed or paused for production click-driven nav

items.title: Sliding Pill
items.description: A 3-segment Day/Week/Month toggle where a glowing translucent pill slides between options with a spring bounce on a dark rounded bar.
items.useWhen: Calendar views, date-range pickers, or any 2-4 option segmented control on dark UI surfaces.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (colors hardcoded for dark surface). More than 4-5 options — pill width math gets cramped.

preserveElements: requestAnimationFrame initial move() call (sizes glide on load)
knownLimitations: Auto-cycles by default — remove loop() call and stop() handler before shipping in production
Background and colors hardcoded for dark surfaces only
No aria-pressed or role=tablist semantics for screen readers

items.title: Breadcrumb Trail

items.useWhen: Navigation demos, onboarding flows, or any UI showing hierarchical page location with a polished dark-mode aesthetic.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background layouts (background hardcoded dark navy). Production breadcrumbs — remove the auto-cycle loop and wire real navigation links.

knownLimitations: Auto-cycles by default — remove the loop() call for production use
Background color hardcoded to dark navy; incompatible with light themes without CSS edits
Button elements lack aria-current on the active crumb — add aria-current='page' for production

items.title: Morphing Bottom Nav

items.avoidWhen: Desktop-only layouts. Auto-cycles by default — remove the loop before shipping as a real nav component.

items.title: Command Palette

items.title: Aurora Borealis

items.title: Mesh Gradient

items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (hardcoded dark base). Layouts requiring legible text overlays — blobs may compete with contrast.

items.title: Particle Constellation

items.title: Film Grain

items.title: Floating Orbs

items.title: Digital Rain

items.useWhen: Dark-mode hero backgrounds, hacker/cyberpunk themes, loading screens, tech brand splash pages.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background UIs (background hardcoded near-black). Contexts where constant column motion competes with foreground content.

items.title: Layered Waves

items.title: Cursor Trail

Live mic mode requires HTTPS or localhost for getUserMedia
No reduced-motion fallback for live mic — draws one static frame then stops

items.title: Circular Wave

items.title: Stroke Draw-On

items.description: Radial spotlight follows the pointer, punching through a dark veil to reveal a pink dot grid and glowing word. Auto-demos via Lissajous path when idle.

items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (veil color hardcoded dark navy). Contexts where cursor visibility matters — cursor is hidden inside the canvas.
items.agentPrompt: Change the revealed word by setting WORD='HELLO', shift accent to electric blue by setting ACC='#00eaff' and updating all rgba(255,92,168,...) calls to match.
preserveElements: evenodd arc clip for outer veil
knownLimitations: Background and veil color hardcoded dark navy — not theme-switchable without editing multiple rgba values
cursor:none hides the system cursor inside the tile — must restore in production wrappers
Dot grid loops over full canvas each frame; dense grids on large screens may drop frames

items.title: Elastic Ring
items.description: Glowing pink ring trails the pointer with damped-spring physics, stretching along velocity and squashing across it. Auto-demos on a Lissajous path when idle.
items.useWhen: Creative portfolio heroes, cursor-replacement effects, interactive showcases where pointer physics add tactile feel.
items.avoidWhen: Utility UIs where a hidden cursor frustrates users. Touch-only devices where pointermove never fires.
items.agentPrompt: Change ACC to '#00eaff', increase base ring size from Math.min(W,H)*0.085 to 0.12, and slow the spring by changing the acceleration factor from 0.14 to 0.08.
knownLimitations: Cursor is hidden (cursor:none) — must restore for production if replacing only part of a page
No touch fallback — demo auto-plays but ring never tracks finger on touch devices

items.title: Trail Comet
items.description: Hot-pink comet spring-follows the cursor, dragging a tapering additive trail that lengthens with speed and flings spark particles. Idle: Lissajous demo loop.
items.useWhen: Dark-background hero sections, creative portfolios, cursor-effect showcases where a premium tactile feel is needed.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (hardcoded dark fill). Forms or text-heavy layouts where a custom cursor competes with reading.
items.agentPrompt: Change comet color to cyan: replace all 'rgba(255,92,168,...)' with 'rgba(0,234,255,...)' and update the hot-core stop from rgba(255,210,235,...) to rgba(200,255,255,...).
knownLimitations: Background hardcoded to #0a0a0f — change both CSS and ctx.fillStyle to use on light pages
Reduced-motion renders one static frame, no pointer interactivity

items.title: Ripple Field
items.description: Grid of dots ripples outward in expanding pink rings as the pointer moves; dots displace radially along each wavefront. Lissajous auto-demo when idle.
items.useWhen: Dark-mode hero backgrounds, interactive landing sections, ambient pointer-reactive displays where motion follows the cursor.
items.avoidWhen: Low-end devices — per-frame dot displacement loop scales with grid size. Light backgrounds require CSS overrides; avoid where subtle motion is needed.
items.agentPrompt: Change the accent color from pink to electric cyan by setting ACC=[0,234,255], and increase ring expansion speed by changing the ringSpeed multiplier from 0.62 to 0.85.
knownLimitations: Per-frame dot displacement is O(dots * ripples) — heavy on large viewports with many ripples active
Background color hardcoded in CSS (#0a0a0f) — must edit to change from dark navy
No aria semantics — purely decorative canvas

items.title: Scramble Decode
items.description: Gold monospace text resolves from random glyphs left-to-right with a tracking caret, holds with micro-flickers, then re-scrambles through a 6-phrase loop.
items.useWhen: Hacker/terminal aesthetic hero sections, loading screens, or reveal animations where text identity emerges from noise.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring immediate legibility — text is unreadable during scramble. Avoid when motion-sensitive audiences are primary users.

knownLimitations: Phrase cycling is fully automatic — no click-to-advance
BG color hardcoded in both JS constant and CSS; must change both
Cell width factor (0.62) may mis-align without a true monospace system font

items.title: Stagger Rise
items.description: Gold serif letters rise through a clipped baseline one by one, hold as a word, then exit upward in a staggered loop.
items.useWhen: Hero headlines, loading screens, or ambient brand displays needing elegant looping text reveal on dark backgrounds.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (dark navy is hardcoded). Strings over ~12 characters where stagger delay makes last letters slow to appear.

preserveElements: tte02-clip overflow:hidden wrapper (creates the reveal mask)
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in CSS — must edit to use on non-dark backgrounds
Google Fonts (Fraunces) required — falls back to Georgia if offline but weight may differ

items.title: Gradient Wipe

items.useWhen: Dark-mode hero titles, product names, or brand wordmarks needing a premium metallic reveal loop.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (hardcoded dark body). Multi-word strings longer than ~10 chars where the sheen band looks thin.

items.title: Weight Wave

items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (hardcoded dark canvas). Words longer than ~8 characters where wave shape becomes unclear.

items.title: Block Reveal

items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (dark navy hardcoded). More than 3 lines — stagger timing compounds and loop pacing breaks.

items.useWhen: Cyberpunk/hacker aesthetic hero sections, error states, game UIs, or any dark-theme display needing dramatic ambient motion.

No pointer interactivity — purely ambient

items.title: Dot Assemble
items.description: Thousands of colored dots scatter from off-screen and converge to paint a pointillist sunrise scene — hill, sky, and sun disc — then dissolve and loop.
items.useWhen: Hero backgrounds, art/creative portfolio sections, ambient loading screens with a generative art aesthetic.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring legible text overlays or low-end mobile — the pixel-loop dot rasterizer is CPU-intensive at high DPR.
items.agentPrompt: Change the sun color to deep red by swapping PAL indices 0–2 to [200,60,40], move the sun position to the center by setting sunX=W2*0.5 and sunY=H2*0.35, and slow assembly to 5 seconds by changing the 3.4 divisor to 5.
preserveElements: low-res ImageData pixel loop (core perf pattern)
knownLimitations: CPU-bound inner pixel loop — heavy on low-end devices at 2x DPR
Background color hardcoded in both CSS and BG array — must update both to change
No pointer interactivity — purely passive

items.title: Seurat Shimmer
items.description: Up to 2600 colored dots optically mix to paint a pointillist riverbank at dusk — sky, water, and grassy bank zones shimmer via per-dot breathing sine waves.
items.useWhen: Dark-mode hero backgrounds, art/generative portfolios, ambient screensaver-style tiles where painterly texture is the aesthetic.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background UIs (background hardcoded dark navy). Contexts needing text legibility — dense dot field competes with overlaid copy.
items.agentPrompt: Change the scene from riverbank to a sunset beach by shifting PAL entries: set cobalt water to [0,180,220], saffron to [255,140,0], and increase horizon to 0.52.
preserveElements: typed-array + single putImageData blit pattern (critical for perf)
knownLimitations: CPU-bound inner pixel loop — heavy on low-end devices at high dot counts
Background color hardcoded in CSS (#0d0f14) — must edit two places to change ground color
No pointer/touch interactivity — purely passive

items.title: Pointillist Sunset
items.description: A glowing sun sets over shimmering water rendered as colored dot lattices. Sky baked once offscreen; water dots animate with sinusoidal shimmer and sparkle.
items.useWhen: Dark-mode hero backgrounds, art or gallery themes, ambient screensavers where a painterly aesthetic fits.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background layouts (background hardcoded dark). Contexts needing full reduced-motion stop — only one static frame renders.
items.agentPrompt: Change the sun palette to cooler pink tones by setting SUN to [255,200,200] and SUN_R to [200,80,120], then shift WATER_W to [180,120,200] to tint reflections lavender.
editableAreas: sun/sky color palette (SKY_HI, SKY_LO, SUN, SUN_R)
preserveElements: offscreen sky canvas baked once and blitted (critical for perf)
typed-array pixel buffer via stampDot (critical for perf)
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded to dark navy (#0d0f14) in CSS
stampDot pixel loop is CPU-heavy at large viewport sizes on low-end devices
No pointer or touch interactivity — purely passive

items.title: Stipple Flow
items.description: 108 colored dots drift along a curling sinusoidal flow field, leaving slow translucent trails that paint a sweeping diagonal arc with divisionist optic-mixing.
items.useWhen: Dark ambient backgrounds, generative art hero sections, creative portfolio loops where painterly motion suits the brand.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (hardcoded dark navy). Contexts requiring legible text overlaid — persistent trails compete with reading.
items.agentPrompt: Change the palette array PAL to cool teals and purples (e.g. [0,200,180],[80,180,220],[140,80,200]), reduce N to 72, and slow the tick to t+=0.002.
editableAreas: flow field shape (curve/swirl coefficients in fieldAng)
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in both CSS and paintBG() — must change both
No pointer interactivity; dots ignore cursor entirely
Static reduced-motion frame uses a separate dense-stipple render path (6500 random dots), not the agent system

items.title: Divisionist Tree
items.description: 2600 orange, blue, and magenta dots form a tree canopy using additive pixel-splat blending; dots vibrate with staggered sine-phase for optical color mixing.
items.useWhen: Dark-background hero sections, generative art displays, nature or botanical themes needing ambient motion without interactivity.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (dark navy hardcoded). Low-end devices — per-frame pixel loop over 2600 dots is CPU-bound.
items.agentPrompt: Change canopy palette to cool purples and greens: set PAL indices 0-2 to [160,80,220],[120,60,200],[80,40,180] and rerun.
knownLimitations: Per-frame pixel loop over up to 2600 dots — CPU-bound on low-end mobile
Background color hardcoded to #0d0f14 in both CSS and BG array — must change both
No interactivity — purely autonomous

items.title: Density Wave
items.description: A density wave sweeps left to right revealing a Divisionist sunset — sky, sun disc, and sea rendered as jittered pigment dots that optically mix at distance.
items.useWhen: Ambient hero backgrounds with a painterly aesthetic. Gallery intros, creative portfolio headers, screensavers.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring readable text overlay or low-end mobile — per-frame ImageData pixel loop is CPU-bound and background is hardcoded dark.
items.agentPrompt: Change sunX to 0.65 and sunY to 0.48 to move the sun right, then swap SKY palette to cool blues/greens for a dawn scene.
preserveElements: typed array cell buffers (cellR/G/B/JX/JY/Sz/Thr) — allocation-free inner loop
knownLimitations: Per-frame full ImageData pixel write is CPU-bound — can drop frames on large viewports on low-end devices
Background color hardcoded dark navy in CSS — must edit to change
No pointer interactivity — purely autonomous

items.title: Halftone Bloom
items.description: Colored dots bloom radially from center, optically mixing amber, magenta, and teal into a glowing flower. Slow ripple breathes through the dot grid.
items.useWhen: Dark-mode hero backgrounds, loading screens, or ambient art tiles needing organic radial motion without user interaction.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (hardcoded dark navy). Layouts needing legible text overlay — dot field competes with copy.
items.agentPrompt: Change the palette to cool blues by replacing PAL values and slow the bloom cycle by setting PERIOD to 8000.
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in both CSS and canvas fillRect — must change in two places
Dot count capped at 110 regardless of screen size, so large viewports show coarser grid

items.title: Confetti Mosaic
items.description: 104 confetti dots fly in from the edges, settle into three color bands (amber/teal/plum), then shimmer with sine-wave opacity.
items.useWhen: Decorative hero backgrounds or splash pages that benefit from an energetic build-in followed by calm ambient motion.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring legible text overlays or light-mode UIs — background is hardcoded dark.
items.agentPrompt: Change BANDS to sunset palette (warm red, orange, gold base colors) and slow settle duration from 2600 to 4000ms.
editableAreas: dot radius range (0.012..0.022 of min dimension)
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded to #0d0f14 — edit CSS and paintBg to change
Resize restarts the intro animation from scratch

items.title: Pointillist Rain
items.description: 104 stippled dots fall as rain, splashing into elliptical ripple rings on an amber puddle against a divisionist dark sky.
items.useWhen: Ambient dark-mode backgrounds, seasonal/weather-themed hero sections, generative art displays.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (sky hardcoded dark navy). Low-end devices where many canvas draws per frame may stutter.
items.agentPrompt: Change DROP palette to cool blues only, increase rain count N from 104 to 160, and slow fall speed by changing rv upper bound from H*0.026 to H*0.016.
preserveElements: offscreen bg canvas (buildBg) — critical for perf
knownLimitations: Background color and sky palette hardcoded — changing theme requires editing multiple arrays
Ripple ring buffer fixed at 10 slots; heavy rain may recycle rings too fast
No pointer interactivity — purely passive

items.title: Vibrato Field
items.description: 108 paired complementary dot-couples oscillate apart so the eye optically fuses them into a shimmering amber-over-cobalt vibrato across a dark field.
items.useWhen: Dark-mode hero backgrounds, art/design portfolio ambience, or anywhere a painterly, slowly-shifting texture is needed without distraction.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (hardcoded dark navy), contexts requiring legible text overlays, or low-end mobile where 216 canvas arcs per frame may stutter.
items.agentPrompt: Change the dominant pair to teal/rose by replacing PAIRS[0] with ["#2dd4bf","#e11d74"] and slow the oscillation by changing t*1.6 to t*0.9.
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in both CSS and JS fillRect — must change in both places
No touch/pointer interactivity; purely passive
Additive blending can blow out to white on very bright color pairs

items.title: Diagonal Thrust
items.description: Red and black arrow-tipped slabs sweep along a -28° diagonal axis over a black field; a dark-red disc and a bold type bar anchor the composition.
items.useWhen: Hero backgrounds evoking bold graphic-design or avant-garde aesthetics. Dark-mode landing pages, art/design portfolio headers.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background UIs (background hardcoded near-black). Contexts requiring legible text overlays — slabs cross the full canvas.
items.agentPrompt: Change the diagonal angle from -28° to -45° by setting ANG = -45*Math.PI/180, and increase slab count to 8 by adding two new entries to the SLAB array.
editableAreas: slab definitions (SLAB array — offset, length, thickness, color, speed, phase)
palette constants (RED, RED2, INK, PAPER, CREAM)
disc position and size (-0.66 multiplier, 0.072*S radius)
knownLimitations: Background hardcoded to near-black (#0c0c0d) — must change CSS and BLACK constant together
Type bar is a decorative shape only — no real text rendered
Slabs wrap via modulo; very slow speeds may produce visible stutter at wrap boundary

items.title: Red Wedge
items.description: Red wedge drives into a recoiling black disc, scattering shards; bold slab type fades in after impact. Looping Constructivist poster animation.
items.useWhen: Hero backgrounds or loading screens for art, design, or cultural contexts; constructivist/avant-garde themed projects needing ambient tension.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring legible content overlays or conservative UIs where the political art reference is unwanted.
items.agentPrompt: Change the wedge color to deep orange by setting --cv02-red to #e65c00 and --cv02-red-deep to #a83f00, then slow the cycle to 6s by updating the animation duration on all cv02-* keyframe rules.
editableAreas: animation cycle duration (4.6s on all classes)
preserveElements: prefers-reduced-motion guard (static fallback shows fully-set composition)
knownLimitations: All timing sync relies on shared 4.6s duration across CSS classes — must update all simultaneously if changed
Shard positions are hardcoded in SVG units and may clip on very narrow screens

items.title: Rotodynamic
items.description: 18 slab bars and word spokes radiate from an off-center hub, rotating at staggered speeds in a Rodchenko red-black-bone palette.
items.useWhen: Dark-mode hero backgrounds, editorial/art-direction contexts, motion posters, or any canvas needing bold geometric ambient animation.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (hardcoded dark palette). Contexts where constant rotational motion competes with primary content or text legibility.
items.agentPrompt: Change spoke count from N=18 to N=12, replace WORDS entries with English words like 'POWER','BUILD','MOVE', and halve all spokeRate values to slow rotation.
knownLimitations: Background and palette are hardcoded — must edit constants to change from dark constructivist scheme
Text on spokes flips on the left half but readability still degrades at acute angles

items.title: Floating Forms
items.description: Suprematist field: tilted black square anchors red and off-white bars, squares, and a dot drifting in slow staggered CSS cycles on near-black.
items.useWhen: Art/design portfolio heroes, dark-mode ambient backgrounds, editorial splash screens with a modernist or Bauhaus aesthetic.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background layouts (background hardcoded near-black). Contexts requiring full motion stop — reduced-motion collapses animations but shapes stay tilted.
items.agentPrompt: Change the accent color from red (#d11f1f) to cobalt blue (#1a4fd1) on all .cv04-red and #cv04-glow elements, and slow the anchor drift to --dur:18s.
editableAreas: individual shape sizes and positions (left/top/width per ID)
breathe opacity floor (0.62 in cv04-breathe keyframe)
preserveElements: CSS custom properties driving per-shape drift (--r, --dx, --dy, --dr, --dur, --del)
knownLimitations: Background color is hardcoded in both html/body and #cv04-stage — must edit two places to change it
Static red glow is percentage-positioned and may drift visually off-center on extreme aspect ratios

items.title: Megaphone
items.description: A Constructivist megaphone emits expanding concentric red and paper-white arc rings across a dark field with bold diagonal bars. Runs autonomously.
items.useWhen: Dark hero backgrounds evoking Soviet-era propaganda aesthetics, art/design portfolio showcases, bold ambient motion for creative brands.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background layouts (background hardcoded dark). Contexts requiring legible text overlays — the expanding rings compete with content.
items.agentPrompt: Change the ring accent from red to gold (#c8a000), slow the spawn interval by setting spawnGap to 1.8, and widen the shout cone by increasing spread from 0.78 to 1.1.
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in CSS and JS — must change in both places
Ring color alternation tied to radius bucket, not index — changing N shifts the pattern
No pointer interactivity; composition is fixed

items.title: Gear Poster
items.description: Three SVG gears spin at different speeds while red diagonal bars slam in and slab-type text assembles — all on a dark constructivist grid.
items.useWhen: Industrial, retro, or constructivist-themed hero sections, loading screens, or ambient poster displays.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background UIs (bg hardcoded dark). Contexts where constant looping text assembly is distracting to readers.
items.agentPrompt: Change the poster words to 'FORGE' and 'WORKS', set --cv06-red to #e87722, and slow the bar slam cycle to 5s.
preserveElements: prefers-reduced-motion guard in CSS and JS
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in CSS — must edit --cv06-bg to change from near-black
Gears are decorative and do not actually mesh at correct tooth ratios

items.title: Montage Cut
items.description: 11 Constructivist cut-outs slide in along diagonal angles then hold; a bold MONTAGE type stack and a slashing red diagonal anchor the composition.
items.useWhen: Art/design portfolio heroes, editorial layouts with a retro avant-garde aesthetic, or loader screens needing a bold geometric assembly moment.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring legible text overlays or light backgrounds — background is hardcoded dark near-black and the composition is dense.
items.agentPrompt: Change the accent color from red (#d11f1f) to deep orange (#e65c00) by updating the RED constant, slow the assembly cycle to 6000ms by changing CYCLE=4200 to CYCLE=6000, and increase the diagonal bar width from S*0.085 to S*0.11.
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in CSS and JS — must change in both places
Halftone dot loop redraws every frame — mildly expensive at large canvas sizes
Type always appears after g>0.55 with no separate delay control

items.title: Type Stack
items.description: Three rows of bold slab letters (TYPE / STACK / 1923) slide and pop along a tilted red diagonal bar; a white square ticks back and forth across the axis.
items.useWhen: Constructivist or brutalist hero backgrounds, retro design showcases, editorial motion posters.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring legible text — letters are decorative and stagger-animate. Avoid on light backgrounds without CSS edits.
items.agentPrompt: Change the words to 'FORM / SPACE / 2024', swap the red axis color to deep blue (#1a237e), and slow the cycle to 9s.
knownLimitations: Font falls back to Impact/system-ui — no variable-font axis, so weight is fixed at 900
Background color hardcoded in CSS variable --cv08-bg; must edit root to change dark theme

items.title: Crane Build
items.description: A Constructivist tower crane sways and hoists a red block while a stack builds on a foundation; bold red wedge, white band, and amber rule compose the backdrop.
items.useWhen: Constructivist or industrial-themed hero sections, portfolio backgrounds, or motion displays needing bold geometric storytelling.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background UIs (background is hardcoded near-black). Contexts where legible text overlays are needed — the crane and stacking blocks compete for attention.
items.agentPrompt: Change the jib color from RED to AMBER and increase crane swing amplitude from 0.07 to 0.14 to make the sway more dramatic.
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in both CSS and JS — must change in both places
Stacked block count resets cyclically every 4 blocks, not a true progressive build
No pointer interactivity — purely passive

items.title: Dynamic Flag
items.description: A red banner waves and snaps along a black diagonal strut, with a white slab bar, rhythm tick marks, and a Malevich-style square accent on a dark ground.
items.useWhen: Constructivist or propaganda-art themed hero sections, political/historical editorial, bold ambient backgrounds needing strong diagonal composition.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (hardcoded dark ground), contexts requiring legible text overlays over the flag area, or low-end devices where the per-frame blit + wave math may stutter.
items.agentPrompt: Change the banner color to deep blue by setting RED='#1a3bd1' and RED2='#122a9e', increase segment count to 20 (SEG=20), and slow the wave speed by changing t*4.0 to t*2.5.
preserveElements: offscreen groundCv cache pattern (prevents gradient alloc in rAF)
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded to dark near-black — must edit BG and CSS to change
No touch or pointer interactivity — purely passive
White slab bar does not clip to banner edges on extreme wave amplitudes

items.title: Squiggle Parade
items.description: Six bold sine-wave ribbons with marching color beads drift across a confetti field of dots, zigzags, crosses, and terrazzo flecks on deep indigo.
items.useWhen: Memphis/80s-retro hero backgrounds, section dividers, or ambient loops needing bold geometric energy without user interaction.
items.avoidWhen: Content-heavy layouts where high-contrast ribbons compete with text. Light backgrounds — BG is hardcoded deep indigo.
items.agentPrompt: Change SQ_N to 8, set the accent color PAL[0] to '#ff00cc', and increase sqAmp range to 0.08+r()*0.10 for taller squiggles.
preserveElements: mulberry PRNG seeding (consistent layout across reloads)
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in CSS and JS — must change both
Confetti layout is static per seed; resize does not re-randomize positions
26-step polyline squiggle may look angular at very large viewport widths

items.title: Terrazzo Scatter
items.description: 96 colored terrazzo chips breathe apart from a tilted slab card and resettle in a 5.2s looping cycle, using a Memphis 80s palette on dark plum.
items.useWhen: Memphis/retro-themed hero backgrounds, dark-mode landing pages, generative art canvases needing ambient motion without user interaction.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (BG hardcoded dark plum). Contexts requiring legible text overlaid on the chips — chips cover the slab fully when settled.
items.agentPrompt: Change the chip count to 64 by setting N=64, swap the palette accent to hot orange by replacing '#36d6c3' with '#ff6a00', and slow the cycle to 8000ms by changing CYCLE=8000.
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in CSS and JS — must change both BG variable and #141019 CSS to retheme
Chip positions seeded deterministically — same layout every load (no randomness per session)

items.title: Zigzag Bounce
items.description: 7 stacked chevron bars in clashing Memphis colors spring upward with a staggered ripple phase; confetti speckles and squiggle accents frame the scene.
items.useWhen: Memphis or 80s-retro themed hero sections, loading screens, or decorative backgrounds needing bold color-blocked motion.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring calm or neutral aesthetics; low-end devices where multiple simultaneous CSS animations may stutter.
items.agentPrompt: Change bar count to 5 by setting N=5, trim chevHeights and phases arrays to match, and swap --mp03-teal to #7c3aed for a purple accent.
knownLimitations: Background color is hardcoded in CSS variable --mp03-bg; must edit both :root and body background to change it
Chevron silhouette is a fixed clip-path polygon — altering zigzag sharpness requires changing polygon points manually

items.title: Confetti Pop
items.description: Memphis geometric shapes burst upward with gravity and rotation in repeating pops on deep plum. Dots, zigzags, triangles, bars, and pluses auto-cycle.
items.useWhen: Celebration screens, launch announcements, festive hero backgrounds with an 80s geometric aesthetic.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts needing legible overlaid text or calm motion — bursts are frequent and high-energy. Low-end mobile with limited CPU.
items.agentPrompt: Replace COL with ["#ff2d78","#00c9ff","#ffe600","#ff6b00","#ffffff"] and set nextBurst = ts + 280 + rnd()*200 to tighten burst frequency.
knownLimitations: Fixed pool of 104 particles — heavy overlap recycles live particles early causing flicker
Background hardcoded in both CSS and JS clearBg(); change both to retheme
No touch/click interaction — purely passive

items.title: Polka Shuffle
items.description: Brick-offset grid of dots, rings, and squares sways and breathes while colors cross-fade between three clashing Memphis 80s palettes on dark plum.
items.useWhen: Retro/Memphis-themed backgrounds, 80s-inspired hero sections, ambient looping pattern displays.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring legible text overlays or calm minimal aesthetics. Low-end devices may struggle with ~110 shapes animating continuously.
items.agentPrompt: Change the palette cycle speed by setting per=6.0, add a fourth palette entry to PALS, and increase the orbital sway multiplier from 0.16 to 0.25 for more chaotic movement.
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded as BG='#141019' — must change both CSS and JS variable to retheme
Ring shape punches out center with BG fill, breaking on non-matching backgrounds

items.title: Grid Jumble
items.description: 5x5 grid of Memphis shapes — circles, triangles, squiggles, zigzags — jittering in sine-wave phases over a terrazzo-speckled dark background.
items.useWhen: Memphis or retro 80s themed hero backgrounds, pattern tiles, or ambient motion displays that need visual density without user interaction.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring text legibility over the canvas. Low-end devices running many simultaneous canvas tiles.
items.agentPrompt: Change COLS and ROWS to 7 for a denser grid, swap PAL accent to '#ff00cc' for neon magenta, and slow wobble by reducing spd range from 0.6+rnd()*1.3 to 0.3+rnd()*0.7.
knownLimitations: Shape variety fixed at 7 types — adding new shapes requires extending the shape() if-else chain
Terrazzo speckle layout tied to seeded RNG (seed=1337) — change seed for a different arrangement
Background color hardcoded in both CSS and JS (BG='#141019') — must update both to change it

items.title: Noodle Loop
items.description: Five thick snaking SVG noodles in Memphis colors travel via stroke-dashoffset on a dark violet field; corner dots pulse and confetti spins.
items.useWhen: Memphis or retro 80s hero backgrounds, screensavers, or decorative section breaks where looping ambient motion fits the brand.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background layouts (background hardcoded dark violet). Content-heavy contexts where thick animated strokes compete with readable text.
items.agentPrompt: Change the teal noodle to hot orange (#ff6b00) by updating --mp07-teal, and slow its travel by setting --mp07-dur to 10s on the fourth path element.
editableAreas: travel speed per noodle (--mp07-dur on each path)
stroke width (stroke-width 13 on the noodle group)
dot pulse scale (1.35 in mp07-pulse keyframe)
preserveElements: IIFE wrapper with setMotion() reduced-motion guard
coral noodle stroke-width:0 markup guard (promoted in JS only when motion allowed)
knownLimitations: stroke-dashoffset is paint-layer — may stutter on low-end devices with all 5 noodles active
Background hardcoded dark violet; update --mp07-bg and gradients for light themes
Coral noodle invisible if JS disabled (stroke-width promoted from 0 only in JS)

items.title: Triangle Rain
items.description: 90 Memphis-palette triangles — solid and outlined — tumble and sway downward through faint dotted columns on a deep plum background.
items.useWhen: Retro/Memphis-themed hero backgrounds, 80s design showcases, ambient looping tile backdrops.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (hardcoded dark plum). Contexts needing legible text overlays — constant motion competes with reading.
items.agentPrompt: Change triangle count to 60 by setting N=60, swap palette to pastels (edit COL array), and slow fall speed by changing the vy base from 0.55 to 0.25.
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in both CSS and canvas backdrop() — must change both to retheme
No interactivity; pointer attraction would require significant refactor

items.title: Shape Stack
items.description: 8 clashing Memphis shapes stack into a gently wobbling totem over a terrazzo background, crowned by a yellow squiggle. Glow baked into offscreen sprites.
items.useWhen: Memphis/retro-themed hero sections, loading screens, or decorative panels needing bold ambient motion without user interaction.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring text legibility over the canvas or low-end devices where glow sprites may stress GPU compositing.
items.agentPrompt: Change the totem's wobble speed by setting the frequency multiplier from 1.15 to 0.7 in drawFrame, and swap COL.orange to '#00eaff' for a cooler palette.
editableAreas: layer definitions (L array — shape types, sizes, phases)
preserveElements: offscreen sprite baking pattern (buildSprites — critical for perf)
knownLimitations: Glow is baked into offscreen sprites at resize — changing GLOW at runtime requires a resize trigger to rebuild
Terrazzo speckle is 6 hardcoded dots via CSS radial-gradient, not truly random

items.title: Pattern Clash
items.description: Three Memphis pattern bands — dots, zigzags, squiggles, triangles, rings — scroll in opposing directions at different speeds, split by crisp accent seams.
items.useWhen: Memphis or 80s retro themed hero backgrounds, pattern showcases, decorative section dividers with high visual energy.
items.avoidWhen: Content-heavy layouts where text must be legible over the background. Low-end mobile where continuous canvas redraws may stutter.
items.agentPrompt: Change the top band accent color from '#36d6c3' to '#00ff99', slow the top scroll speed from 78 to 48, and increase cell size divisor from 6.2 to 5 for larger motifs.
editableAreas: motif kind assignments per band (kindA/kindB args)
knownLimitations: No interactivity — purely passive; adding pointer response requires significant rework
Cell size is viewport-width-relative so very wide screens may show oversized motifs
Background color hardcoded in both CSS and JS BG variable — must update both

items.title: Liquid Melt
items.description: 7 lava-lamp blobs drift and merge with cycling pink-to-cyan hues on a dark canvas; "MELT" lettering warps per-glyph with skew and scale transforms above.
items.useWhen: Dark-themed hero sections, psychedelic or creative portfolio backgrounds, ambient screensaver-style displays.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (canvas bg hardcoded dark), content layouts requiring legible text overlays, low-end mobile (pixel loop per frame).
items.agentPrompt: Change the word from 'MELT' to 'FLUX', shift hue range to 60+90 (yellow-to-green band) by editing the `hue = 260 + 90*Math.sin(hp)` line, and increase blob count NB from 7 to 10.
editableAreas: letter warp intensity (0.16 scale, 10px translateY, 5deg skewX)
preserveElements: low-res 88x88 canvas upscaled via CSS (perf critical)
knownLimitations: Pixel loop runs every frame — CPU-bound on low-end devices
Background color hardcoded to #0a0612 in CSS
No touch/pointer interactivity — purely passive

items.title: Kaleidoscope
items.description: 9 liquid blobs drift in a source wedge mirrored into 6-fold kaleidoscope symmetry on canvas; the whole disc rotates slowly with a hot-pink/violet/amber palette.
items.useWhen: Dark-background hero sections, loading screens, ambient psychedelic or music-festival aesthetics.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background UIs (background hardcoded #0a0612). Layouts where content overlaps the canvas — the constant motion competes with reading.
items.agentPrompt: Change SEG from 12 to 16 for 8-fold symmetry, swap PAL first entry to [0,220,255] for a cyan-dominant palette, and slow the global spin to t*0.00004.
preserveElements: offscreen source canvas pattern (perf-critical — never draw wedge onto visible canvas)
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in CSS (#0a0612) — must edit to change
Blob shapes are polygons with wobble, not true bezier curves — visible at low blob counts
No touch/pointer interactivity — fully passive

items.title: Warp Type
items.description: Bold serif "WARP" sliced into vertical columns that ripple and bulge over swirling magenta-teal lava blobs rendered with additive screen blending.
items.useWhen: Psychedelic or retro-poster hero sections, music/festival visuals, full-bleed ambient backgrounds needing dramatic motion.
items.avoidWhen: Low-end mobile (per-frame offscreen drawImage slicing is CPU-bound). Layouts requiring legible, stable text.
items.agentPrompt: Change the word to 'FLUX', set slice width to 4 for smoother warp, and shift the blob palette to warm amber tones by replacing palette[0] and palette[2].
preserveElements: offscreen text cache (off canvas rebuilt on resize)
blob gradient transform pattern (setTransform scale trick)
knownLimitations: Per-column drawImage slicing is CPU-bound — slice=6 means ~100+ draw calls per frame at full width
Font fallback to Georgia/Times means appearance varies if system fonts differ
No pointer/touch interactivity

items.title: Swirl Vortex
items.description: 5 petal arms across 11 rings rotate at differential speeds to create a slow inward vortex in pink-violet-cyan hues with a glowing core.
items.useWhen: Dark-mode hero backgrounds, psychedelic/creative portfolio sections, ambient looping screensavers.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (hardcoded dark navy). Content-heavy layouts where motion competes with text legibility.
items.agentPrompt: Change ARMS to 7 and shift the hue sweep from pink-cyan to green-gold by setting ringHue[i]=t*120+60 and updating the core gradient stops to rgba(255,220,50,0.9).
preserveElements: trailing-fade fill pattern (rgba fill instead of clearRect)
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in CSS and JS — must change in both places
No pointer interactivity — purely passive
Trail persistence means color changes take several seconds to fully appear

items.title: Blob Lamp

items.title: Ripple Bloom

items.description: Julia set fractal whose complex constant traces a slow Lissajous loop, morphing violet-indigo filaments between connected and dendritic forms over ~70 seconds.
items.useWhen: Dark hero backgrounds, generative art displays, screensavers, or anywhere that needs slow ambient complexity without user interaction.
items.avoidWhen: Low-end mobile devices (CPU-bound pixel loop at up to 420px resolution). Light-background layouts — background is hardcoded near-black.
items.agentPrompt: Change the palette to warm amber/red tones by replacing the stops array (e.g. [[20,5,0],[100,30,10],[220,80,20],[255,140,60],[255,210,130],[255,255,200],[80,40,10]]) and slow the morph by changing 0.00009 to 0.00005.
editableAreas: Lissajous loop center and radii (constantAt function)
preserveElements: low-res internal canvas cap (SW/SH capped at 420px long edge)
Uint32Array pixel buffer pattern (allocation-free in steady state)
knownLimitations: CPU-bound pixel loop — each frame iterates up to MAXIT=150 times per pixel; expensive on slow CPUs
Background and glow colors hardcoded — must edit both CSS vars and LUT stops to retheme
No interactivity — complex constant path is fixed; user cannot steer or zoom

items.title: Game of Life
items.description: Conway's Game of Life with glowing violet cells aging through three color tiers; field reseeds automatically when population collapses.
items.useWhen: Ambient backgrounds for tech/generative-art themes, screensavers, or loading screens needing emergent organic motion.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring legible text overlays or light-background UIs — background is hardcoded dark navy and cell flicker competes with content.
items.agentPrompt: Change palette to green-on-black: set BG=[5,10,5], TIERS=[[80,200,80],[120,255,120],[200,255,200]], and slow step rate to STEP_MS=180.
preserveElements: low-res ImageData stretch pattern (critical for perf)
knownLimitations: Age tracking resets on cell death — settled patterns rarely reach tier-2 color
Background color hardcoded in both CSS and BG array — must update both to retheme
No pointer interactivity; fully passive

items.title: Lenia
items.description: Lenia smooth-life automaton: glowing indigo-to-teal blobs drift and pulse on a dark field via a typed-array convolution sim scaled up with CSS.
items.useWhen: Ambient hero backgrounds for science, generative art, or creative tech themes. Dark-mode showcases needing organic, living motion.
items.avoidWhen: Low-end CPUs (toroidal convolution loop runs every frame). Contexts needing light backgrounds or legible text overlaid on the canvas.
items.agentPrompt: Change MU to 0.18 and SIG to 0.025 to shift blob behavior toward tighter, faster-pulsing clusters, and update the ramp stops to warm amber tones.
knownLimitations: O(N^2 x KLEN) convolution per frame — heavy on low-end devices at larger grid sizes
Grid resolution fixed at 96x96; increasing N sharply raises CPU cost
No interactivity — blobs cannot be steered or seeded at runtime

items.title: L-System Fern
items.description: A fractal fern draws itself branch by branch via an L-system grammar, coloring segments violet-to-mint by depth; it holds then restarts.
items.useWhen: Nature-themed hero backgrounds, generative art showcases, algorithmic illustration demos.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts needing instant full visuals — 3.4s draw cycle means content appears progressively. Avoid on light backgrounds (hardcoded dark theme).
items.agentPrompt: Change the palette to warm amber/orange tones by replacing PAL with ['#7a2f0a','#b84b1a','#e06530','#ff9b5b','#ffcba0','#ffe0c7','#fff0e8','#ffffff'] and slow growth to 5 seconds by changing GROW_PER_SEC to SEGN/5.
preserveElements: pre-computed typed Float32Array segments (critical for perf)
knownLimitations: DEPTH=5 is near the cap of 9000 segments; increasing to 6+ will silently truncate at the hard cap
Background color is hardcoded dark navy — must edit both CSS and radial-gradient to change theme

items.title: Domain Warp
items.description: Slowly flowing domain-warped fractal noise rendered in violet, indigo, and ink tones; layered fbm warp passes produce marbled smoke patterns.
items.useWhen: Dark-mode hero backgrounds, creative portfolio ambience, loading screens needing slow organic motion.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring legible text overlays — the shifting noise competes with content. Light-color UIs (background hardcoded dark near-black).
items.agentPrompt: Change the palette stops array to warm amber/magenta tones (e.g. replace stops with [[6,4,2],[46,20,10],[120,60,20],[220,120,40],[255,180,80],[255,230,200]]) and slow the warp by changing qx multiplier from 0.018 to 0.008.
editableAreas: simulation grid cap (cap=140, lower for speed)
domain warp strength (4.0 multipliers in fbm calls)
preserveElements: low-res offscreen buffer pattern (critical for perf)
knownLimitations: CPU-bound nested fbm loops — 4 octaves x 5 fbm calls per pixel; heavy on low-end devices
Simulation grid capped at 140px edge — upscaled so sharp edges are intentionally soft
No interactivity; purely ambient

items.title: Sierpinski
items.description: Sierpinski triangle accretes from 26,000 chaos-game points, glowing violet on near-black; recent points flash white-hot before cooling.
items.useWhen: Dark-mode hero backgrounds, generative art showcases, screensaver-style ambient displays with a mathematical aesthetic.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background UIs (background hardcoded near-black). Contexts needing immediate full image — triangle builds over many frames before settling.
items.agentPrompt: Change palette to cyan/teal: set COLD=abgr(80,200,200,255), WARM=abgr(160,240,240,255), HOT=abgr(230,255,255,255) and update pip background to #a0f0f0.
preserveElements: drawStatic reduced-motion fallback with seeded RNG
knownLimitations: Animation auto-stops when MAXPTS reached — resize resets and restarts
Background color hardcoded in both CSS and JS buffer fill — must change both
No interactivity; pointer position has no effect on the chaos game

items.title: Elementary CA
items.description: Elementary CA rows scroll upward, aging live cells from bright violet to deep indigo. Auto-cycles through Rule 30, 90, 110 and other interesting rules.
items.useWhen: Dark-mode ambient backgrounds, generative/math-themed hero sections, screensavers, or any context evoking emergence and complexity.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (hardcoded dark), contexts needing legible text overlays, or low-end devices where per-pixel ageGrid updates may stutter.
items.agentPrompt: Change the accent palette to amber — set HOT=[255,220,100], MID=[255,170,50], COOL=[180,100,20] and slow the step rate to STEP_MS=120.
preserveElements: ring-buffer head index logic in render()
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in CSS — must edit both html/body and #fr08-stage to change
Per-pixel ageGrid loop is O(COLS*ROWS) every frame — heavier than it looks on large screens
No user interaction — purely ambient; cannot be wired to clicks without adding event listeners

items.title: Fractal Split
items.description: Mandelbrot left, Julia right, split by a glowing violet seam; Julia parameter orbits smoothly and the palette drifts through indigo and lilac hues.
items.useWhen: Dark-mode hero backgrounds, screensavers, generative-art showcases, or any context wanting ambient mathematical beauty with zero interaction required.
items.avoidWhen: Light backgrounds (dark navy hardcoded), low-end mobile (per-frame pixel loop over 40k pixels), or layouts needing legible text overlays on the canvas area.
items.agentPrompt: Change MAX from 46 to 80 for sharper fractal detail, and shift the palette hue to warm amber by replacing the stops array with [[6,7,11],[74,34,10],[200,110,30],[255,180,80],[255,230,180],[255,250,240]].
preserveElements: low-res offscreen pixel buffer pattern (critical for perf)
knownLimitations: Per-frame pixel loop over 40k pixels — heavy on low-end CPUs; no WebWorker offload
Resolution capped at 200x200 and upscaled pixelated — sharp but blocky look is intentional
Background color hardcoded in CSS — must edit in two places to fully change it

items.title: Langton’s Ant
items.description: Langton's ant crawls a 121x121 toroidal grid, tracing violet highways with a glowing recency trail. Auto-resets after ~14000 steps for an eternal loop.
items.useWhen: Ambient generative art backgrounds, fractal/automata showcases, creative coding demos requiring low CPU draw.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts needing immediate visual clarity — the emergent highway pattern takes thousands of steps to form.
items.agentPrompt: Change palette to amber: set pal to [[80,50,10],[160,100,20],[230,160,40],[255,220,100]] and update BG/C0 to warm dark tones.
knownLimitations: Grid fixed at 121x121 — increasing N raises per-frame cost
Auto-reset at 14000 steps interrupts mid-highway patterns
Background hardcoded in both CSS and BG array — must update both to retheme

items.title: Sankey Flow
items.description: Three-stage Sankey diagram on dark canvas: bezier ribbons grow stage-by-stage from origins to hubs to outcomes, pulsing softly in opacity to show flow volume.
items.useWhen: Data visualization contexts needing animated flow diagrams — traffic splits, funnel analysis, budget allocation, network routing dashboards.
items.avoidWhen: Light-color UIs (background hardcoded dark navy). Contexts needing real data binding — values are synthetic constants in the A/B matrices.
items.agentPrompt: Change the flow data by editing matrix A to [[8,2,1],[1,7,2],[2,1,9]] and matrix B to [[5,4,1,1],[1,6,2,2],[1,1,5,5]], then update COL[0] to '#ff6b6b' for a warm accent.
editableAreas: flow value matrices A and B
knownLimitations: Flow data is hardcoded — no external data binding without refactor
Background color hardcoded in BG and CSS — must change both to retheme
Staged reveal resets on a 9s loop with no pause control

items.title: Chord Diagram
items.description: Eight ring nodes with curved ribbons that swell and shrink as weights rebalance every ~3s. Additive blending creates glowing color overlaps.
items.useWhen: Ambient data-viz backgrounds, network relationship storytelling, dashboards needing a living connections motif.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts needing readable, accurate data — weights are synthetic and auto-cycling. Not suitable as a real chart without wiring actual data.
items.agentPrompt: Change node count to 6 by setting N=6 and trimming PAL to 6 colors, then slow rebalance from t>3.2 to t>6.0 for a calmer pace.
knownLimitations: Data is synthetic — no real data binding; replace seed/rng logic to display actual values
Additive blend mode looks wrong on light backgrounds
No labels or tooltips — purely decorative

items.title: Treemap
items.description: 7 rounded cells partition the canvas via slice-dice treemap; cells lerp to new sizes every 2.6 s as synthetic values drift, with a subtle alpha pulse.
items.useWhen: Dashboard hero tiles, data-viz demos, or ambient backgrounds needing a structured chart feel without real data wiring.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring real data input or interaction — values are synthetic and auto-cycling only; no click or hover API exposed.
items.agentPrompt: Change N to 5 categories, set period to 4000 for slower transitions, and swap --ch03-c0 to #ff6b6b for a warm red accent.
knownLimitations: Labels (A-G) are alphabetic indices only — no real data binding
Slice-dice algorithm can produce tall slivers for extreme value imbalances
Background color hardcoded via --ch03-bg CSS variable; must override to change

items.title: Candlesticks
items.description: Green and red OHLC candles stream in from the right with smooth slide animation; newest candle grows from pivot. Dashed last-price marker traces horizontally.
items.useWhen: Fintech dashboards, trading UIs, ambient data-viz backgrounds where live market feel is needed.
items.avoidWhen: Real data display without replacing the synthetic random-walk generator. Light-colored backgrounds — colors are hardcoded for dark themes.
items.agentPrompt: Change UP color to '#f0b429' and DN to '#e05c6e', increase visible candle count to 36 by setting N=36, and slow the streaming to slideDur=1600.
knownLimitations: Synthetic random-walk data only — no real data binding
Background hardcoded dark (#0a0d12); must edit CSS to change
No Y-axis price labels rendered

items.title: Streamgraph
items.description: Six translucent stacked bands breathe around a central baseline via layered sines; a sweeping readout line tracks across with colored tick dots per band.
items.useWhen: Dashboard hero sections, data visualization backgrounds, ambient charts illustrating trends without real data.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring real data binding or accurate axis labels — values are synthetic sine waves only.
items.agentPrompt: Change COL to warm sunset tones, increase N to 8 bands, slow the sweep by changing tt*0.13 to tt*0.07.
knownLimitations: Data is synthetic sine waves only — no real data binding
Sweep line disabled under prefers-reduced-motion
Background color hardcoded in --ch05-bg CSS variable

items.title: Sparkline Grid
items.description: Nine teal sparklines in a 3x3 grid scroll leftward every 140ms; orange/blue diamonds mark running max/min and a halo dot tracks the latest value.
items.useWhen: Dashboards, monitoring UIs, or ambient data-display tiles needing real-time multi-series charting without a charting library.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring interactive tooltips or labeled axes — this is display-only with no hover state or readable tick marks.
items.agentPrompt: Change grid to 2x2 (set COLS=2, ROWS=2), slow the update interval to 300ms (STEP=300), and shift the accent color to electric blue (#00d4ff).
preserveElements: typed Float32Array ring buffers (allocation-free after setup)
knownLimitations: Synthetic data only — no real data feed wired in
No tooltips or value labels; not screen-reader accessible beyond the aria-label summary
All 9 series share one ring-buffer head, advancing in lockstep

items.title: Bubble Pack
items.description: 64 mint/teal/amber/coral circles settle under gravity with collision physics; size encodes value. Large bubbles pull toward center with a breathing pulse.
items.useWhen: Data visualization contexts needing a lively proportional chart — portfolio breakdowns, category distributions, or ambient dashboard backgrounds.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring labeled or interactive data points — bubbles have no text labels and no click/hover handlers. Avoid on low-end devices if many redraws are expected.
items.agentPrompt: Change bubble count to 80 by setting N=80, swap PAL array to blues/purples palette, and slow breathing animation by changing the 0.0012 multiplier to 0.0006.
knownLimitations: O(N^2) pairwise collision loop — keep N at or below 110 cap or perf degrades
No text labels on bubbles — not suitable as a standalone labeled chart
Background color hardcoded in both CSS and JS fillRect — must change both to retheme

items.title: Waterfall
items.description: Waterfall chart with staggered bar reveals and dashed connectors. Green bars for gains, pink for losses, with a scan dot tracing the running total.
items.useWhen: Finance dashboards, earnings breakdowns, P&L summaries, or any demo showing incremental contribution to a total.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts needing user interaction with chart data, or when more than ~8 bars are needed — bars shrink below readability on narrow viewports.
items.agentPrompt: Change the STEP data to a new P&L scenario: update label/value pairs in the STEP array, set UP to '#60a5fa' for blue gains, and slow the stride to 0.55.
editableAreas: bar data (STEP array — labels, values, types)
knownLimitations: Bar labels can overlap on narrow screens with many steps — no responsive label truncation
Total bar value is implicitly computed from cumulative sum; hardcoding a different total requires updating size() logic
No tooltip or hover interaction — values appear only as inline chips above bars

items.title: Arc Timeline
items.description: Mint playhead sweeps a quarter-circle arc, lighting 11 event markers and tracing a cumulative magnitude ribbon on dark navy.
items.useWhen: Data storytelling dashboards, project retrospectives, or ambient displays showing how events accumulate over a time span.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background UIs (background hardcoded dark navy). Contexts needing interactive scrubbing — playhead sweeps automatically only.
items.agentPrompt: Change accent color from '#5ad1a0' to '#7c3aed', increase event count N to 16, and slow the sweep duration DUR from 8200 to 12000.
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in CSS — must edit --ch09-bg to change from dark navy
No user scrubbing; playhead is purely auto-animated
Seeded random events are fixed — changing N requires re-tuning the seed loop

items.title: Heat Calendar
items.description: GitHub-style 52-week contribution heatmap on dark background; cells pop in column by column as a bright leading edge sweeps left to right, then loops.
items.useWhen: Activity dashboards, developer portfolio heroes, data-viz ambient backgrounds where a looping calendar rhythm adds visual interest.
items.avoidWhen: Real data display without wiring in actual values — synthetic seed data is hardcoded. Light-background UIs (background is hardcoded dark navy).
items.agentPrompt: Change accent color to purple by setting --ch10-acc and --ch10-l3 to #a855f7, update --ch10-l4 to #d8b4fe, and slow the sweep by changing DUR from 4200 to 7000.
knownLimitations: Synthetic deterministic data — must replace val[] generation to show real contribution data
Background color hardcoded in CSS; must edit to use on light themes
No tooltip or interactivity on hover over individual cells

items.title: Stamp Button
items.description: A neo-brutalist yellow button auto-stamps with a chunky red offset shadow — the box shifts down-right to simulate a physical press on a 2.5s loop.
items.useWhen: CTA or submit button demos, neo-brutalist UI kits, landing pages needing bold tactile button feel.
items.avoidWhen: Production forms — the stamp loop auto-cycles; remove animation and wire a real click handler before shipping.
items.agentPrompt: Change the button label to 'BUY NOW', swap the shadow color to #00c8ff, and slow the stamp cycle to 4s by editing the animation duration in .bru-btn.bru-animate.
knownLimitations: Auto-cycles by default — must remove loop and add click handler for production use
cursor:default prevents pointer affordance; change to cursor:pointer for interactive version

items.title: Newspaper Ticker
items.description: Two rows of bold uppercase headlines scroll in opposite directions with red accent highlights — pure CSS marquee in neo-brutalist black-and-cream style.
items.useWhen: News tickers, event dashboards, or portfolio hero banners needing scrolling text with editorial urgency.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring readable static text — continuous scroll makes sustained reading impossible.
items.agentPrompt: Change row 1 speed to 12s (edit `.bru-t1` animation duration), swap accent color from #ff2d00 to #0055ff, and add a third ticker row.
preserveElements: duplicate span blocks (required for seamless 50% translate loop)
knownLimitations: Each track requires content duplicated exactly twice for the seamless loop — adding spans must be mirrored
Reduced-motion users see static non-scrolling headlines with no animated fallback

items.title: Error Manifesto
items.description: A neo-brutalist error card with hard shadow and border cycles its text color between white, red, and yellow in stepped CSS keyframe flashes.
items.useWhen: Error states, 404 pages, or brutalist-themed dashboards needing dramatic status callouts.
items.avoidWhen: Polished or minimal design systems — the hard-shadow aesthetic and color strobing clash with refined UI. Avoid where flashing colors are an accessibility concern.

knownLimitations: Stepped color cycling may be considered distracting or inaccessible in contexts with photosensitive users even with reduced-motion off
No click or hover interaction — purely decorative auto-cycle

items.title: Receipt Card
items.description: A neo-brutalist receipt card with lime-green monospace text on black; line items fade in one-by-one with a staggered delay, revealing a running total.
items.useWhen: Order confirmations, checkout summaries, or brutalist-themed UI demos that need a structured itemized reveal.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background pages (background hardcoded #111). Real-time or interactive checkout flows — items and prices are static.
items.agentPrompt: Change the order items and prices in the `rows` array, update the `totals` array to match running sums, and set the accent color from #b8ff57 to #ff4d4d.
editableAreas: row items and prices (rows array + HTML span text)
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded to #111 — must edit CSS to use on light pages
Items and prices are static HTML — not data-driven from an API

items.title: Warning Tape
items.description: Two scrolling yellow-black hazard stripe bands frame a bold "UNDER CONSTRUCTION" label that gently pulses. Pure CSS, zero JS logic.
items.useWhen: Maintenance pages, coming-soon placeholders, neo-brutalist hero sections needing high-contrast industrial energy.
items.avoidWhen: Polished, minimal, or light-background UIs — background is hardcoded dark (#111) and aesthetic is intentionally abrasive.

label subtitle text (DO NOT CROSS THIS LINE)
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded #111 — must edit CSS to change dark surround
Stripe scroll direction is fixed left-to-right; reversing requires negating background-position end value

items.title: Data Grid
items.description: A neo-brutalist 5-row data grid showing node load % and status; cells flash cyan, lime, or red every 800ms to reflect ERR/WARN/OK states.
items.useWhen: Server dashboards, system monitors, or any data-heavy brutalist UI needing live-updating tabular status indicators.
items.avoidWhen: Light or minimal design systems — the hard black borders and flat neon flashes are intentionally jarring. Not suitable for accessibility-critical contexts without ARIA roles.

No ARIA roles on cells — not screen-reader friendly
Flash clear at 300ms can race with the next 800ms tick if interval is shortened

items.title: Shell Session
items.description: Simulates a looping shell session: commands type out character-by-character with a blinking cursor, then output lines appear, then the next command begins.
items.useWhen: Developer portfolios, tech landing pages, retro/hacker aesthetic backgrounds, loading screens for CLI-adjacent tools.
items.avoidWhen: Non-technical audiences or contexts without a dark/terminal theme. Avoid where motion competes with primary content.

knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded in CSS (#0d1117) — must edit to change from dark navy
Line cap at 18 removes oldest lines silently with no scroll history

items.title: Resource Monitor
items.description: Retro BBS-style system monitor showing 6 CPU core bars and 3 memory rows with ASCII block characters; values drift randomly every 420ms.
items.useWhen: Terminal/hacker aesthetic dashboards, loading screens, or ambient system-status displays in dark-mode UIs.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background layouts (foreground and background are hardcoded dark orange-on-black). Do not use where real system data is expected.

DOM rebuild on every tick (innerHTML reset) — acceptable at low frequency but not scalable to high-frequency updates

items.title: Log Stream
items.description: Simulates a live server log tail — new entries slide in every 650ms with color-coded INFO/WARN/ERROR/DEBUG levels and real-time timestamps.
items.useWhen: Developer dashboards, terminal-aesthetic hero sections, ops-themed loading screens or ambient backgrounds.
items.avoidWhen: Light-background UIs (background hardcoded dark navy). Contexts where motion competes with readable content.

preserveElements: prefers-reduced-motion static fallback (fills lines without animating)
knownLimitations: Background color hardcoded dark — must edit CSS to change theme
No click interactivity — purely ambient
Timestamps use local clock so they jump on tab switch

items.title: Build Runner
items.description: Simulates npm install + build pipeline in a scrolling terminal; packages, lint/compile steps, and file-change events stream in sequence and loop endlessly.

Loops forever with no pause control exposed to the user

items.title: Network Trace
items.description: Simulates a live traceroute — hops appear one by one with color-coded latency (green/orange/red); ms values fluctuate after trace completes, then loop.
items.useWhen: Terminal-aesthetic dashboards, network/devops themed hero sections, or ambient tech backgrounds.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring real network data — all hops are hardcoded fiction. Not suitable as an actual diagnostic tool.

Grid column widths hardcoded; long hostnames truncate silently

items.title: Hex Viewer
items.description: Simulates a live hex dump viewer — rows of ELF/binary data scroll up with address offsets, hex bytes, and ASCII columns; new rows fade in every 550ms.
items.useWhen: Hacker/terminal aesthetic backgrounds, developer tool UIs, loading screens for CLI-style apps.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring calm or minimal motion — the constant row insertion is distracting. Light-background UIs (colors hardcoded for dark).

No reduced-motion animation pause — only fills static rows once, no fallback visual indicator

items.title: Soft Button
items.description: Circular neumorphic play/pause button in a soft inset ring. Click depresses it with inset shadows and toggles icon/label between PLAY and PAUSE.

Auto-cycles every 2s by default — remove setInterval for production

items.title: Toggle Switches

items.title: Progress Track

items.title: Volume Knob

items.useWhen: Audio/music UI mockups, settings panels, or any neumorphic design showcase needing a realistic dial component.
items.avoidWhen: Contexts requiring actual user input — the knob is display-only with no drag/click interaction wired up.

items.title: Stat Cards
items.description: Four neumorphic stat cards (temp, humidity, power, signal) cycle through live-updating values one at a time, each card pulsing its shadow on update.

items.title: Ring Gauge
